tools.name: get_cost_and_usage
tools.description: Retrieve AWS cost and usage data with optional filtering and grouping. Returns cost breakdown by time period with grand total. Use get_today_date first to determine correct date ranges. Use the MINIMUM calls needed: for a simple spend question, ONE monthly call with no group_by. Add group_by (SERVICE/REGION) only for a breakdown; use DAILY only for trends. Call get_today_date first to ground relative dates. Do not run extra queries the user didn't ask for.

end_date.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format (EXCLUSIVE - use first day of next period)

metrics.description: Cost metrics: UnblendedCost, BlendedCost, AmortizedCost, etc. (default: [UnblendedCost])

group_by.description: Dimensions to group by: SERVICE, REGION, LINKED_ACCOUNT, USAGE_TYPE, etc.

filter.description: Cost Explorer filter Expression (optional). A nested boolean of Dimensions/Tags/CostCategories with And/Or/Not. Example: {"Dimensions": {"Key": "SERVICE", "Values": ["Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud - Compute"]}}. Common keys: SERVICE, REGION, LINKED_ACCOUNT, USAGE_TYPE, RECORD_TYPE.

tools.name: get_cost_and_usage_comparisons
tools.description: Compare costs between two time periods to identify changes and trends. Returns per-service comparison with change amounts and percentages.

tools.name: get_cost_forecast
tools.description: Generate cost forecasts based on historical usage patterns.

start_date.description: Forecast start date (YYYY-MM-DD, must be in the future)

tools.name: get_dimension_values
tools.description: Get available values for a Cost Explorer dimension (e.g., list all SERVICE names, REGION names).

dimension.description: Dimension name: SERVICE, REGION, LINKED_ACCOUNT, USAGE_TYPE, etc.

tools.name: get_tag_values
tools.description: Get available values for a specific cost allocation tag key.

tag_key.description: The tag key to get values for

tools.name: start_query_execution
tools.description: Execute SQL on CUR (Cost and Usage Report) 2.0 data via Athena. Use 'cur_data' as the table name (auto-substituted). ALWAYS include partition filters for performance: billing_period = 'YYYY-MM' is the ONLY partition column (string). Date columns are TIMESTAMP type — use date_format(bill_billing_period_start_date, '%Y-%m') = 'YYYY-MM' for monthly, or line_item_usage_start_date >= TIMESTAMP 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' for daily. NEVER use LIMIT (use max_results). Key fields: line_item_unblended_cost, line_item_usage_account_id, line_item_product_code, line_item_line_item_type, line_item_resource_id, resource_tags (cost-allocation tags: element_at(resource_tags, 'user_<tag>')). For amortized cost, CASE on line_item_line_item_type: SavingsPlanCoveredUsage -> savings_plan_savings_plan_effective_cost; DiscountedUsage -> reservation_effective_cost; RIFee -> reservation_unused_amortized_upfront_fee_for_billing_period + reservation_unused_recurring_fee; else line_item_unblended_cost. Exclude Marketplace with bill_billing_entity = 'AWS'; exclude Tax/Credit/Refund via line_item_line_item_type NOT IN (...).

query_string.description: SQL query to execute. Use 'cur_data' as table name. Example: SELECT line_item_product_code, SUM(line_item_unblended_cost) FROM cur_data WHERE year='2026' AND month='02' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

tools.name: get_enrollment_status
tools.description: Check if Cost Optimization Hub is enabled for this account. Call this FIRST before other COH tools — if not enrolled, other calls will fail.

tools.name: list_recommendations
tools.description: List cost optimization recommendations (right-sizing, idle resources, Savings Plans, Reserved Instances). Sorted by estimated savings. Supports filtering by resource type, action type, region.

resource_types.description: Filter by resource types (e.g., Ec2Instance, LambdaFunction, EbsVolume)

action_types.description: Filter by action types (e.g., Rightsize, Stop, Delete, MigrateToGraviton)

tools.name: get_recommendation
tools.description: Get detailed information for a specific recommendation by ID.

recommendation_id.description: The recommendation ID to look up

tools.name: list_recommendation_summaries
tools.description: Get aggregated savings summaries grouped by a dimension (ResourceType, Region, AccountId, ActionType). Useful for high-level views like total savings by resource type.

group_by.description: Dimension to group by: ResourceType, Region, AccountId, ActionType, ImplementationEffort (default: ResourceType)

tools.name: get_health_events
tools.description: Query AWS health events with flexible filters. Supports filtering by account, service, status, region, risk level, event scope, and date range. Use days_back for relative date queries. Pass account_id to use the AccountTimeIndex (fast). Pass only time filters to use the CategoryTimeIndex (fast). Service-only queries scan. Returned events include narrative enrichment fields when available: impactSummary (one-sentence summary), remediationHint (operator action), affectedResourceTypes (normalized resource tokens) — prefer these over the raw description for concise user-facing responses.

service.description: Filter by AWS service (e.g., EC2, RDS, Lambda)

status.description: Filter by status: open, closed, upcoming

risk_level.description: Filter by risk: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW

event_scope.description: Filter by scope: PUBLIC (widespread AWS issue), ACCOUNT_SPECIFIC (affects your resources), NONE

tools.name: get_events_by_account
tools.description: Get all health events for a specific AWS account ID.

days_back.description: Only events from last N days

tools.name: get_events_by_service
tools.description: Get health events for a specific AWS service.

days_back.description: Only events from last N days

tools.name: get_critical_events
tools.description: Get CRITICAL and HIGH risk health events, sorted by most recent.

tools.name: get_recent_events
tools.description: Get most recent health events from the last N days, sorted by update time.

tools.name: get_event_summary
tools.description: Get summary counts of health events grouped by status, service, region, risk_level, event_scope, or category.

group_by.description: Group by: status, service, region, risk_level, event_scope, category (default: status)

days_back.description: Only include events from last N days

tools.name: get_event_by_arn
tools.description: Get a single health event by its ARN.

event_arn.description: Full ARN of the health event (required)

tools.name: get_anomalies
tools.description: Get cost anomalies detected by AWS Cost Anomaly Detection. Returns unusual spending patterns with impact amounts.

days_back.description: Number of days to look back for anomalies (default: 30)

tools.name: get_billing_alerts
tools.description: Get active AWS Budgets and their current status — shows budget limits, actual spend, forecasted spend, and utilization percentage.

tools.name: get_account_info
tools.description: Get basic account billing info — account ID, aliases, organization membership, and account name.

tools.name: list_services
tools.description: List available AWS services in the pricing catalog. Returns service codes and their filterable attributes.

tools.name: get_service_pricing
tools.description: Get on-demand pricing for a specific AWS service. Use filters to narrow by region, instance type, OS, etc. Returns per-unit USD pricing.

service_code.description: AWS service code (e.g., AmazonEC2, AmazonRDS, AmazonS3, AWSLambda)

filters.description: Key-value attribute filters as a flat {string: string} map. Common keys shown below; any AWS pricing attribute is accepted. Example: {"regionCode": "us-east-1", "instanceType": "m5.xlarge", "operatingSystem": "Linux"}.

tools.name: get_attribute_values
tools.description: Get valid values for a service attribute. Use this to discover filter options before calling get_service_pricing.

attribute_name.description: Attribute to get values for (e.g., instanceType, regionCode, operatingSystem)

tools.name: get_today_date
tools.description: Return today's UTC date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Call this before date-sensitive queries to ground the agent in the current day.

tools.name: discover_dx_topology
tools.description: Discover the caller's AWS Direct Connect topology across all reachable regions. Returns a TopologyData JSON (DX connections, virtual interfaces, DX gateways, Transit Gateways, VPCs, VPNs, Cloud WAN) plus a fetchErrors list describing any partial API failures. Idempotent within a conversation — call ONCE per session unless the user explicitly asks to refresh. For a credential-free demo, pass mock_scenario (noResiliency | devTest | high | maximum | crossAccount | cloudWan) to return a fixture topology instantly.

default_region.description: Home region to seed multi-region discovery from. Defaults to the Lambda's AWS_REGION.

mock_scenario.description: Optional: return a fixture topology instead of calling AWS. One of: noResiliency, devTest, high, maximum, crossAccount, cloudWan. Useful for demos without live credentials.

tools.name: assess_dx_resiliency
tools.description: Run the 5 resiliency rules + 17 best-practice checks + 2 SLA-precondition attestations against a topology. Returns {topology, assessment} — the topology that was assessed PLUS the CombinedAssessment (perDxGateway, global, resiliency, bestPractice). The visualizer uses both. If topology is not passed, the tool fetches it first. Call after discover_dx_topology (or instead of it) when the user asks about resiliency, best practices, or recommendations. Resiliency is reported PER DX GATEWAY as a tier (none/devtest/high/maximum) — there is NO single account-wide score; never invent one. Self-contained: omit topology to fetch live, or pass mock_scenario for a deterministic demo. NEVER fabricate SLA %, tier names, or BGP limits.

topology.description: Optional TopologyData (as returned by discover_dx_topology). Omit to fetch live or pass mock_scenario.

targets.description: Target tier. Pass a string 'high' or 'maximum' to apply to every DXGW, OR an object keyed by DXGW ID mapping specific targets (missing keys default to 'high'). Defaults to 'high'. AgentCore gateway schema doesn't support oneOf — the handler accepts either shape.

default_region.description: Used only when topology is not passed.

mock_scenario.description: Used only when topology is not passed. One of: noResiliency, devTest, high, maximum, crossAccount, cloudWan.

tools.name: get_recommendation_details
tools.description: Re-run the assessment and return a single recommendation by ID (title, description, severity, ghost-node specs). Cheaper than re-sending the full assessment when the user asks about one specific recommendation.

recommendation_id.description: ID from a prior assess_dx_resiliency result (e.g., 'bp-bgp-route-limit' or 'rec-single-dx-location-dxgw-001').

topology.description: Optional cached topology. Omit to re-fetch.

targets.description: Same semantics as assess_dx_resiliency.targets — pass a string 'high'/'maximum' OR an object keyed by DXGW ID.

tools.name: get_dx_pricing
tools.description: Look up live AWS Direct Connect port pricing + data-transfer rate for a region and port speed via the AWS Pricing API. Returns monthly cost per connection, total for num_connections, and USD/GB outbound data-transfer rate. Use this only for DX-specific pricing — for generic AWS service pricing, defer to the pricing-agent sibling.

port_speed.description: DX port speed. Must be one of: 1Gbps, 10Gbps, 100Gbps.

tools.name: estimate_upgrade_cost
tools.description: Estimate the additional monthly cost to move the topology from its current tier to 'high' or 'maximum'. Combines topology shape (location/device gap) with live DX pricing. Rough budgeting estimate only — excludes data transfer, cross-connect fees, and partner pricing nuances.

target_tier.description: Target resiliency tier. One of: 'high', 'maximum'. Defaults to 'high'.

port_speed.description: Port speed to use for cost modeling. One of: 1Gbps, 10Gbps, 100Gbps. Default 10Gbps.

region.description: Region for pricing lookups. Inferred from topology connections if omitted.

tools.name: get_required_tags
tools.description: Resolve the required-tag policy from either the caller's input or the AWS Organizations Tag Policy attached to the current account. Returns {required_tag_keys, allowed_values, case_sensitive, source}. If the caller supplies required_tags, uses those; otherwise reads the Organizations Tag Policy. Returns an error with a starter-tag hint if neither source is available.

required_tags.description: Optional caller-supplied list of required tags. Either a flat list of key strings (e.g. ['Environment', 'Owner']) or a list of objects with per-key allowed values (e.g. [{'key': 'Environment', 'allowed_values': ['prod', 'dev']}]). Omit to read from AWS Organizations Tag Policy. Items are tag-key strings; for per-key allowed values pass the allowed_values map instead.

allowed_values.description: Optional map of tag_key -> list of permitted values. Overrides any embedded allowed_values in the required_tags parameter. Shape: an object mapping each tag key to an array of permitted string values, e.g. {"Environment": ["prod", "dev", "staging"]}.

tools.name: list_tag_keys_in_use
tools.description: Return every tag key currently applied to at least one resource in the caller's account and region. Use this when the operator has no formal policy and wants to see what they're already tagging with before writing one. Output can seed a required_tags list for check_tag_compliance.

region.description: Optional AWS region to query. Omit to use the Lambda's default region.

tools.name: check_tag_compliance
tools.description: Scan AWS resources and report tag compliance. Two evaluation modes: (1) In-Python classifier (default) uses AWS Resource Explorer as the inventory source and requires a resolved required-tag policy (caller-supplied or Organizations Tag Policy). (2) AWS server-side evaluation (set use_aws_evaluation=true) uses ResourceGroupsTaggingAPI GetResources with IncludeComplianceDetails and requires an attached AWS Tag Policy; caller-supplied required_tags is ignored in this mode. Returns summary counts (compliant/non_compliant/compliance_pct in default mode), breakdowns by violation type and by required tag, top-10 worst-offending resource types, accounts, and regions, a capped list of non-compliant resources with their specific violations, and a complete remediation_buckets array (aggregated counts per (account, region, resource_type, tag_key, violation_type)) that survives the max_resources cap and is the preferred input to get_remediation_guidance. By default excludes system-managed resource types (SageMaker trial components, default parameter groups, etc.) that operators can't practically tag. If Resource Explorer is not indexed and default mode is requested, returns a clear 'resource_explorer_not_indexed' status. SOURCE OF TRUTH: required tags come ONLY from (1) an attached AWS Organizations Tag Policy (used when required_tags is omitted) or (2) tags the user explicitly names (pass via required_tags). NEVER invent tag keys; 'Environment'/'Owner'/'Project' are illustrative, not defaults. If no policy is attached and the user named no tags, ask which tags to check rather than guessing.

required_tags.description: Optional: same format as get_required_tags. Omit to use the Organizations Tag Policy. Ignored when use_aws_evaluation=true. Items are tag-key strings; for per-key allowed values pass the allowed_values map instead.

allowed_values.description: Optional tag_key -> allowed values map. Ignored when use_aws_evaluation=true. Shape: an object mapping each tag key to an array of permitted string values, e.g. {"Environment": ["prod", "dev", "staging"]}.

use_aws_evaluation.description: Set to true to use AWS server-side evaluation via ResourceGroupsTaggingAPI GetResources(IncludeComplianceDetails=True). Requires an attached AWS Tag Policy. Default false uses the in-Python classifier with Resource Explorer.

regions.description: Optional list of regions to scan when use_aws_evaluation=true. Omit to use the Lambda's default region. Ignored in default mode (Resource Explorer is cross-region via its aggregator).

resource_types.description: Optional filter, e.g. ['ec2:instance', 's3:bucket']. Omit to scan all resource types.

max_resources.description: Max non-compliant resource entries to include in the detail list (default 25, cap 1000). Summary counts are always complete.

include_system_managed.description: Set to true to include system-managed resource types (SageMaker experiment trial components, default memorydb/rds parameter groups, AWS-managed SSM documents, etc.) that are normally excluded as unactionable noise. Default false.

tools.name: get_org_tag_compliance_summary
tools.description: Aggregated tag-compliance counts via ResourceGroupsTaggingAPI GetComplianceSummary. Returns counts only (no ARNs, no tags) grouped by TARGET_ID, REGION, or RESOURCE_TYPE — for dashboards and executive rollups. Requires an attached AWS Tag Policy (NoTagPoliciesAttached error otherwise) and management-account access. IMPORTANT: these numbers are only as complete as the Tag Policy configuration — if the policy doesn't cover all required tags / all resource types / use the enforced_for reporting clause, real non-compliance will be undercounted. The response includes an accuracy_caveat field that the calling agent MUST surface to the user. For per-resource drill-down use check_tag_compliance instead.

group_by.description: Dimensions to group by: TARGET_ID (account), REGION, RESOURCE_TYPE. Default ['TARGET_ID'].

account_ids.description: Optional filter by AWS account IDs.

tools.name: find_untagged_resources
tools.description: Find resources with zero tags via Resource Explorer 'tag:none' query. GetResources explicitly does NOT return zero-tagged resources, so this is the only way to surface them. Paginates up to 1000 results per call (Resource Explorer's server-side cap). When a single resource_type or region is passed, it is pushed into the query for server-side filtering so the cap is less likely to bite. Returns api_limit_reached=true with a hint when the 1000 ceiling is hit. Degrades gracefully with 'resource_explorer_not_indexed' status if the aggregator index isn't configured.

account_ids.description: Optional filter by AWS account IDs (applied post-query).

regions.description: Optional filter by AWS regions. A single region is pushed into the Resource Explorer query server-side; multiple regions are filtered post-query. Use 'Global' for global services (IAM, CloudFront, Route 53).

resource_types.description: Optional filter by resource type (e.g. 'iam:role'). A single value is pushed into the Resource Explorer query server-side; multiple values are filtered post-query.

limit.description: Max resources to return (default 1000, capped at 1000 by Resource Explorer's per-query ceiling).

tools.name: list_cost_allocation_tag_status
tools.description: List cost-allocation tag activation status via Cost Explorer ListCostAllocationTags. A tag being policy-compliant does NOT mean it's activated for billing — activation is a separate payer-only action. Surfaces the gap so operators can see which compliant tags are actually usable in Cost Explorer and CUR. Payer-only API; returns a clear error if not run from the management (payer) account.

status.description: Optional filter: 'Active' or 'Inactive'. Omit for all.

type.description: Optional filter: 'UserDefined' or 'AWSGenerated'. Omit for all.

tools.name: get_remediation_guidance
tools.description: Generate AWS Resource Explorer deep-links to bulk-fix non-compliant resources. Prefer passing remediation_buckets from the check_tag_compliance response (aggregated, complete regardless of max_resources) — this guarantees priorities are correct. Falls back to non_compliant_resources (per-resource, may be truncated). Emits one link per (account_id, region, resource_type, tag_key, violation_type) bucket, pre-filtered to exactly those resources. Links are ordered by bucket size (most resources first) so the highest-impact fixes appear first, and are capped at max_links (default 20) with a links_truncated flag when more buckets exist. Operator workflow: open link, select resources, Actions > Manage tags > Add/Edit tag. Commercial AWS partition only. SELF-CONTAINED: if you pass NEITHER remediation_buckets NOR non_compliant_resources, this tool runs check_tag_compliance itself (honoring any required_tags / resource_types / regions / use_aws_evaluation you pass) and generates the links directly — so you can call it standalone without threading a prior scan's output.

remediation_buckets.description: Preferred input: the remediation_buckets array from check_tag_compliance. Each item is {tag_key, violation_type, account_id, region, resource_type, count}. Aggregated server-side, reflects the full scan even when non_compliant_resources is truncated.

non_compliant_resources.description: Fallback input when remediation_buckets is not available. Array of non-compliant resource entries as returned by check_tag_compliance. May be truncated by max_resources, so bucket priorities could be misleading if the scan was large.

scan_method.description: Either 'resource_explorer' or 'aws_server_side_evaluation'. Used to add a caveat note to the summary (AWS-evaluated scans don't return expected_values for invalid_value violations, so invalid_value links let you inspect current values rather than pre-filter by them).

max_links.description: Maximum number of remediation links to return (default 20). Links are ordered by bucket size descending, so the top-impact buckets are always included. When truncated, the response includes links_truncated=true and links_dropped with the count of buckets not shown.
